title: glyceria × loliacea
intro: Glyceria × loliacea, also known as Manchurian foxtail, is a hybrid plant species that combines characteristics of Glyceria maxima and Lolium temulentum, both native to Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Glyceria × loliacea include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of Glyceria × loliacea typically involve its roots or leaves being dried and used as teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
